subject: Vectrix dashboard outage last night — what happened?

messages.subject: Vectrix dashboard outage last night — what happened?
messages.body:
Hi Priya,

Our team was working late last night (Dec 23) and the Vectrix dashboard went down around 10:30pm PT for roughly 90 minutes. No alerts came through to our Slack integration either — our on-call engineer had no idea there was a problem until a customer reported an issue.

This is the first outage we've experienced since signing up in October. Can someone walk me through what happened and what the plan is?

Thanks,
Kevin Park
Head of Engineering, Brainloop

messages.subject: Out of Office: Vectrix dashboard outage last night — what happened?
messages.body:
Hi,

I'm out of the office December 23 through January 2 with limited email access.

For urgent product or infrastructure issues, please contact Marcus Webb at marcus@vectrix.io.

I'll respond to your message when I return January 3.

Best,
Priya Chandran
CEO, Vectrix Labs

messages.subject: Following up on last night's Vectrix outage
messages.body:
Hi Marcus,

Reaching out directly — Priya is out. Can you fill me in on what happened last night? I need to be able to explain it to our VP.

Main questions:
1. What caused the outage?
2. Why didn't our Slack alerts fire?
3. What changes are coming?

Kevin

messages.subject: Re: Following up on last night's Vectrix outage
messages.body:
Hi Kevin,

Thank you for reaching out, and I apologize for the disruption. Here's the full picture:

Root cause: A rate limiter bug in our deploy at 10:15pm PT caused it to reject all inbound API requests. Jordan caught it at 11:45pm and rolled back the deploy. Service fully restored at 11:58pm — approximately 90 minutes of downtime.

Why your Slack alerts didn't fire: Our alert evaluation runs through the same API layer that was rate-limited. When the limiter blocked requests, alerts stopped evaluating too. This is a design flaw — alert evaluation should be isolated from API traffic. We're fixing it and shipping the fix in our next release (target: Jan 6).

What we're doing:
1. Decouple alert evaluation from the API layer (ships Jan 6)
2. Add pre-deploy smoke tests for rate limiter config changes
3. Formalize on-call rotation — Jordan was the sole engineer on-call Dec 23. That won't happen again.

Formal post-mortem will be in your customer portal by December 27.

Again, I'm sorry. This shouldn't have happened, and the alert failure is particularly unacceptable — that's the thing we're there for.

— Marcus Webb
CTO, Vectrix Labs
